response.justification: While the candidate exceeds the 15-year experience requirement and demonstrates strong technical leadership through staff-level positions and open-source work, the role explicitly requires AI/ML expertise as its core specialization. The resume shows no demonstrated AI product work, ML expertise, or advanced AI education—only a brief 6-month stint with AI infrastructure tooling. For a director-level principal engineer role focused on AI strategy and advising customers on cutting-edge AI technologies, this represents a fundamental skills gap that cannot be offset by general technical excellence.
strengths: 20 years of software engineering experience exceeds requirement; demonstrates sustained career growth from freelancer to staff-level roles
Proven technical leadership through founding GISCollective, organizing workshops, and mentoring (Refactoring Dojos, Selenium courses); shows ability to inspire and educate
Exceptional technical breadth across full-stack, infrastructure, hardware, and multiple languages; comfortable as a 'tool-picker' rather than specialist
concerns: Zero demonstrated expertise in AI/ML despite role requiring 'recognized expertise in AI with demonstrated products or platforms built with AI'
No advanced degree in AI-related field; recent AI work is limited to 6 months of infrastructure/tooling (MCP), not strategic AI product development
Limited cloud platform strategy experience; AWS exposure appears operational (CDK for pricing) not architectural or strategic at platform level
